footer.tagline: Morocco's reference platform for mortgage comparison.

items.q: What is the best mortgage rate in Morocco in 2026?
items.a: Mortgage rates in Morocco in 2026 range from 4.50% to 5.50% for fixed-rate loans, depending on the bank and borrower profile. Attijariwafa Bank and BCP generally offer the most competitive rates for both residents and MREs.

items.q: Can an MRE get a mortgage in Morocco?
items.a: Yes, all Moroccan banks have products dedicated to Moroccans Residing Abroad (MRE). You typically need a convertible dirham account, 3 months of bank statements, and proof of stable income in your country of residence.

items.q: How do I calculate my mortgage monthly payment?
items.a: The formula is M = P × [r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n - 1], where P = principal borrowed, r = monthly rate (annual rate ÷ 12), and n = number of monthly payments. Our simulator performs this calculation automatically with MRH insurance included.

items.q: What is the maximum duration for a mortgage in Morocco?
items.a: The maximum duration is generally 25 years (300 months) for residents and 20 years for MREs. Some banks may go up to 30 years depending on the profile.

items.q: What is a Convertible Dirham Account?
items.a: A Convertible Dirham Account is a Moroccan bank account allowing MREs to freely transfer funds between Morocco and abroad. It is essential for financing real estate in Morocco from abroad.
